case_title: SAN FRANCISCO DEPUTY SHERIFF'S ASSOCIATION VS. KEN LOMBA ET AL

docket_entries.description: DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 60367911) AS TO DEFENDANT AGUERRE, ANTHONY

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KENNETH LOMBA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED IN PART. MR. LOMBA IS AWARDED ATTORNEYS' FEES AND HIS REQUEST FOR COSTS IS GRANTED BASED ON THE MEMORANDUM OF COST. THE AMOUNT OF ATTORNEYS' FEES SOUGHT BY MR. LOMBA IS EXCESSIVE FOR THE RELATIVELY SIMPLE ISSUES INVOLVED IN HIS SUCCESSFUL ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. MR. KATZENBACK TO PREPARE A REVISED PROPOSED ORDER AND A PROPOSED AMENDED JUDGMENT INCLUDING COST AND ATTORNEY'S FEE AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-02-2017 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar May-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. VANCE PIGGOTT OF THE RLS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-916-646-2860 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER W. KATZENBACH OF THE KATZENBACH LAW OFFICES, TEL. NO. 1-415-834-1778 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court modified its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: Defendant Kenneth Lomba's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in part. Mr. Lomba is awarded attorneys' fees and his request for costs is granted based on the memorandum of cost. The amount of attorneys' fees sought by Mr. Lomba is excessive for the relatively simple issues involved in his successful anti-SLAPP motion. The hourly rate of $450 is reasonable. 35 hours is at the high end of the range of reasonable hours to spend on the anti-SLAPP motion: roughly 20 for the moving papers, 10 for the reply papers (although no reply papers were needed) and 5 for the hearing. 20 hours is at the high end of the range of reasonable hours to spend on this fees motion: 10 hours for the moving papers, 5 for the reply papers (although no reply papers were needed) and 5 for the hearing. The court allowed a 1.25 multiplier applied on the ant-slapp motion because merely reading the complaint showed that this case was likely subject to dismissal on an anti-SLAPP motion and the relevant facts were few, essentially undisputed, and uncomplicated. None of the foregoing is to suggest that counsel for Mr. Lomba did not do a good job on the motion. To the contrary, he did do a good job. It just wasn't that difficult of a job. Mr. Katzenback to prepare a revised proposed order and a proposed amended judgment including cost and attorney's fee and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk

Notice Of And Special Motion To Strike Complaint Of San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs Association By Defendant Kenneth Lomba
Off Calendar Mar-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter A. Hoffman, of Rains Lucia Stern, PC, Tel. 925.609.1699, for plaintiffs;
Vance Piggott, of Rains Lucia Stern St. Phalle & Silver, PC, Tel. 916.646.2860, for plaintiffs;
Christopher W. Katzenbach, of Katzenbach Law Offices, Tel. 415.834.1778, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KENNETH LOMBA's Special Motion To Strike the first, third and fourth causes of action in the Complaint Of San Francisco Deputy Sheriffs' Association is granted as to all three causes of action. Mr. Lomba satisfied his first prong burden of the anti-SLAPP analysis by showing that all three claims against him arise from protected activity set forth in CCP 425.16 (e)(1) and (2). Paragraph sixteen of the complaint alleges that Mr. Lomba used the membership list to solicit money from the Association's members. Paragraph twenty-eight of the complaint and the evidence show that Mr. Lomba was using the list to contact and gain support and funds for a lawsuit. (Lomba Dec., par. 15; Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1056 ("the filing, funding, and prosecution of a civil action" is protected activity under CCP 425.16; Dove Audio, Inc. v. Rosenfeld, Meyer & Susman (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 777, 784 (communications in preparation of litigation are protected activity)).The Association's allegations regarding improper solicitation to fund a lawsuit are not merely incidental. (Haight Ashbury Free Clinic, Inc. v.Happening House Ventures, (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1539, 1551, n.7). The Association has not met its second prong burden of showing that there is the requisite minimal merit to any of its claims against Mr. Lomba. The declarations of Eugene Cerbone and John Lawsha do not show that Mr. Lomba took or misused the Association's membership list. The only competent evidence on this issue is Mr. Lomba' declaration which states that he never obtained any sort of membership list from the Association. This evidence shows that there is no merit to any of the Association's claims against Mr. Lomba. Mr. Lomba cannot be liable for violation of Corporations Code 8338 unless he impermissibly used the list. The Association also fails to present any evidence to support the elements of its the breach of fiduciary duty cause of action. The claim for injunctive relief, which is a remedy rather than a true cause of action, fails because it is premised on the section 8338 and fiduciary duty causes of action. Order and judgment signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
